county_metadata.parent_county: Caddo, Bossier, Natchitoches, De Soto, Bienville
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1871; Reconstruction-era formation; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Red River Parish Clerk of Court (Coushatta). Formed 1871 from Caddo, Bossier, Natchitoches, De Soto, and Bienville. Named for the Red River. Coushatta was the site of the Coushatta Massacre (1874) during Reconstruction. Pre-1871 records split among parent parishes. Cotton plantation area. Red River Parish Library has local history. Cross-reference with Natchitoches for colonial-era records. Named also for the Coushatta Native Americans. Protestant and Catholic church records. Anglo-American and Creole heritage. River transportation records at Louisiana State Archives.

source_profiles.source_name: Red River Parish Conveyance Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: Red River Parish Clerk of Court, Coushatta
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Conveyance (deed) books for Red River Parish from 1871 forward; Louisiana uses civil law system — property transfers recorded as notarial acts

source_profiles.source_name: Red River Parish Succession Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: Red River Parish Clerk of Court, Coushatta
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Succession records (Louisiana's civil law equivalent of probate) for Red River Parish; includes inventories, family meetings (conseil de famille), and partition records naming heirs

source_profiles.source_name: Red River Parish Marriage Records — Clerk of Court / Catholic Church Registers
source_profiles.repository: Red River Parish Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil marriage licenses and Catholic church marriage registers for Red River Parish; church records often predate civil registration
source_profiles.survival_notes: Statewide vital records registration from 1914; earlier records at parish level and in church registers

source_profiles.source_name: Red River Parish Church Records — Baptist, Methodist, Presbyterian
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Louisiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Protestant church records for Red River Parish including membership rolls, baptisms, marriages, and burials; supplements civil records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1868; Reconstruction-era formation; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Richland Parish Clerk of Court (Rayville). Formed 1868 from Ouachita, Carroll, Franklin, and Morehouse. Northeast Louisiana delta cotton country. Pre-1868 records split among parent parishes. Rayville area families. Richland Parish Library has local history collections. Anglo-American settlement predominates. Protestant church records (Baptist, Methodist) primary sources. Delhi area families. Boeuf River and Bayou Macon area. Cross-reference with Ouachita and Franklin for earlier records. Cotton plantation records extensive for antebellum period. Named for rich delta soil.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1843; antebellum parish; Central Louisiana
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Sabine Parish Clerk of Court (Many). Formed 1843 from Natchitoches. Western Louisiana borderland — Sabine River forms the Texas border. Named for the Sabine River. Pre-1843 records in Natchitoches. Neutral Ground (No Man's Land) between Spanish Texas and American Louisiana — this lawless zone attracted settlers, outlaws, and refugees. Fort Jesup (1822-1846) records at NARA. Toledo Bend Reservoir area. Sabine Parish Library has local history. Mixed heritage — Anglo-American, Creole, Spanish, and Native American. Zwolle Tamale Festival reflects mixed cultural heritage.

county_metadata.record_losses: Hurricane Katrina (2005) devastated the parish; courthouse flooded, many records damaged or destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Bernard Parish Clerk of Court (Chalmette). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807). BURNED PARISH — Hurricane Katrina (2005) devastated the parish; courthouse flooded and many records damaged or destroyed. Site of the Battle of New Orleans (1815) — Chalmette Battlefield National Historical Park. Isleno (Canary Islander) community settled in the 1770s under Spanish rule — Los Islenos Heritage and Cultural Society has community records. Catholic church registers at St. Bernard Church. Fishing and trapping heritage. Pre-Katrina records at Louisiana State Archives (microfilm copies). Cross-reference with Orleans Parish for related families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; River Road plantation region; significant free people of color community
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Charles Parish Clerk of Court (Hahnville). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807). German Coast — German immigrants settled here in the 1720s under John Law's Company of the West. One of the earliest areas of European settlement in Louisiana outside New Orleans. Destrehan Plantation (oldest documented plantation home in lower Mississippi Valley). Catholic church registers at St. Charles Borromeo (Destrehan) from colonial era. 1811 German Coast Uprising (largest slave revolt in US history) records. Sugar plantation records extensive. Cross-reference with St. John the Baptist for German Coast families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1810; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Florida Parishes (former Spanish West Florida)
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Helena Parish Clerk of Court (Greensburg). Formed 1810 from Spanish West Florida territory. Florida Parishes region — part of Republic of West Florida (1810) before US annexation. Anglo-American settlement predominates. Greensburg area families. Piney woods region. Pre-1810 Spanish colonial records at Louisiana State Archives. Protestant churches (Baptist, Methodist) predominate — church records are essential sources. St. Helena Parish Library has local history. Parent parish for Livingston (1832). Cross-reference with East Feliciana and Livingston. Named for Saint Helena, not for any local geographic feature.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; River Road plantation region
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. James Parish Clerk of Court (Convent). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807). River Road plantation country between New Orleans and Baton Rouge. Major sugar plantation parish with extensive enslaved persons records. St. James Church (Catholic) registers from colonial era. Laura Plantation — Creole plantation museum with documented oral histories of enslaved persons. Oak Alley Plantation area. German Coast settlement. Extensive notarial records documenting slave sales, successions, and property. Cross-reference with St. John the Baptist and Ascension for River Road families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; River Road plantation region
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. John the Baptist Parish Clerk of Court (Edgard). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807). German Coast — among the earliest European settlements in Louisiana outside New Orleans (1720s German immigrants). Site of the 1811 German Coast Uprising (largest slave revolt in US history). Whitney Plantation (Slavery Museum) has extensive enslaved persons documentation. Catholic church registers at St. John the Baptist Church (Edgard) from colonial era. Sugar plantation records. Cross-reference with St. Charles for German Coast families. Reserve and LaPlace area records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; Acadian/Cajun heartland; significant free people of color community
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Landry Parish Clerk of Court (Opelousas). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807) from the Opelousas District. Third-largest parish in Louisiana. Opelousas served as Confederate capital of Louisiana (1862-1863). Major Acadian and Creole settlement. Zydeco music heritage (Creole music tradition). Significant free people of color community in the antebellum period. Catholic church registers at Immaculate Conception (Washington) and St. Landry Church (Opelousas) from colonial era. Opelousas Museum and Interpretive Center. Parent parish for Acadia, Evangeline, and Allen. Extensive notarial records. Cross-reference with Lafayette and Avoyelles.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; Acadian/Cajun heartland; significant free people of color community
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Martin Parish Clerk of Court (St. Martinville). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807) from the Attakapas District. Heart of Acadiana — St. Martinville is known as Le Petit Paris. Longfellow-Evangeline State Historic Site. Major Acadian settlement from the 1760s deportation. Catholic church registers at St. Martin de Tours Church (St. Martinville) — one of the oldest in Louisiana, records from 1756. Acadian Memorial in St. Martinville. Significant Creole and free people of color heritage. Sugar plantation records. Breaux Bridge area (Crawfish Capital of the World). Cross-reference with Lafayette and Iberia for related families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: French and Spanish colonial land grants, US territorial confirmations, and General Land Office patents for St. Martin Parish area; Louisiana State Land Office records

source_profiles.source_name: St. Martin Parish Tax Assessment Rolls — Louisiana State Archives
source_profiles.repository: Louisiana State Archives, Baton Rouge
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Parish tax assessment rolls for St. Martin Parish listing property owners, real estate, and (before 1865) enslaved persons; useful when census records are missing
